{"query":"Wimbledon Park expansion High Court ruling March 2026 presiding judge name","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1678,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.archpaper.com/2025/07/wimbledon-expansion-can-proceed/","title":"Wimbledon can proceed with expansion, High Court judge rules","content":"On July 21, Judge Pushpinder Saini rejected the challenge brought by Save Wimbledon Park (SWP) against the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club (AELTC) in London’s High Court.
After receiving approval from just one of the two necessary boroughs in London, the decision moved to the Greater London Authority (GLA)."},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/wimbledon-expansion-plan-set-to-proceed-after-high-court-ruling/","title":"Wimbledon expansion plan set to proceed after High Court ruling | Courthouse News Service","content":"LONDON (AFP) — Wimbledon’s controversial expansion plan is set to go ahead after a legal challenge against the decision to approve the proposal was dismissed by a High Court judge on Monday.
The “Save Wimbledon Park” campaign group took action against the Greater London Authority’s decision last year to grant a planning permission that would almost triple the size of the site for the grass-court Grand Slam.
The proposals would see the construction of 38 new tennis courts and an 8,000-seat stadium on the grounds of the former Wimbledon Park Golf Club, which would allow it to host All England Club qualifiers on site instead of across south London at Roehampton.
In a ruling on Monday, Justice Saini dismissed the challenge."},{"url":"https://tennisnow.com/high-court-ruling-favors-wimbledon-expansion/","title":"High Court Ruling Favors Wimbledon Expansion – Tennis Now","content":"The grass-court Grand Slam could triple in size after High Court Justice Thompsell shot down a lawsuit filed by Save Wimbledon Park.
Justice Thompsell ruled the land was “never appropriated or dedicated to the use of public recreation” and instead “was used as a private golf club”, adding that the club was “unencumbered by any statutory trust,” according to a Sky Sports published report.
All England Club Chair Deborah Jevans called the ruling “a significant milestone.”
“The ruling represents a significant milestone for our plans, which will, as well as delivering 27 acres of beautiful new public parkland on previously private land, allow us to maintain Wimbledon’s position as one of the world’s most successful sporting events,” Deborah Jevans said in a statement."},{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/5827803/2026/03/19/wimbledon-tennis-expansion-all-england-club-courts-explained/","title":"Wimbledon’s tennis expansion explained: Why new ruling unlocks plans for 39 new courts - The Athletic","content":"Wimbledon’s tennis expansion explained: Why new ruling unlocks plans for 39 new courts
It has been updated to reflect the announcement on March 19, 2026 that the central issue in Save Wimbledon Park’s legal challenge to Wimbledon’s plans is not admissible.
In September 2024, the Greater London Authority (GLA) granted the AELTC permission to go ahead with expansion plans, which included an 8,000-seater show court, on the site of the golf course across the road from the famous ground.
SWP had launched a legal challenge to the plans in January 2025, but in a judicial review that July, Mr Justice Saini ruled that the GLA was lawful in granting planning permission.
Wimbledon currently operates with the minimum number of courts mandated for a Grand Slam tournament (The All England Club)
Then, in a statement dated January 3, 2025, SWP confirmed that it would challenge the permission granted by the GLA at a public hearing on Friday, September 27."}]}

In High Court, Judge Pushpinder Saini rejected Save Wimbledon Park’s challenge to the lawfulness of planning permission, maintaining that a covenant signed by the AELTC when it first acquired the land in 1993 was taken into account. While the ruling provided a thorough interpretation of the planning policy surrounding the expansion plan, the AELTC has been emboldened by the challenge to further underscore the case’s precedent and clear its roadblocks once and for all. “In the circumstances we recognise that the correct thing to do, at this stage, is to put the matter before the court to establish that there is no trust over the land.” A hearing to challenge the existence of the statutory trust has been requested by the AELTC and is scheduled for January 2026.","score":0.6847074,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cwyr7e394p0o","title":"Plans to expand Wimbledon can go ahead, judge rules","content":"In his 31-page ruling, Mr Justice Saini said that the authority \"properly considered the implications of the development on public open space\".","score":0.5660431,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/wimbledon-expansion-plan-set-to-proceed-after-high-court-ruling","title":"Wimbledon expansion plan set to proceed after High Court ...","content":"In a ruling on Monday, Justice Saini dismissed the challenge. “In short, the defendant's decision on the relevance of deliverability, applying to both the","score":0.5473921,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cjag.org/2026/02/10/is-there-a-trust-wimbledon-expansion-returns-to-high-court","title":"Is there a Trust? Wimbledon expansion returns to High Court","content":"Wimbledon Park (SWP) rejecting all grounds in his ruling, AELTC chair Deborah Jevans believes that “there is not, his judgment would carefully","score":0.5411427,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2025/nov/19/wimbledons-development-plans-hit-stumbling-block-after-judges-ruling","title":"Wimbledon's expansion plans heading for court of appeal ...","content":"Lord Justice Holgate had granted a judicial review of the court's decision.","score":0.51662046,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.78,"request_id":"637db9c7-b86c-439a-8a08-c8d0c5eaa793"}
